stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Meta-Llama-3-8B model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under key ML domains including transformers, llama, and text-generation.
This indicates ongoing community interest and benchmarking activity around Meta's latest LLM releases.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The content highlights the trending status of the meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model.
It is categorized under text-generation pipelines, indicating its primary function as a generative AI model.
The associated tags (transformers, llama, safetensors) confirm its relevance within the modern LLM ecosystem.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the 'deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized for text generation tasks, indicating its primary function in conversational AI or content creation.
It is associated with key ML technologies like transformers and safetensors, placing it within the modern LLM ecosystem.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The content highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under core NLP/ML tags like 'transformers' and 'pytorch'.
The pipeline indicates the model is being used for text generation tasks.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, now integrates MLX for performance optimization.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon hardware, enhancing local AI execution on Macs.
The feature is currently available in a preview build, indicating ongoing development and optimization.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, ensuring all processing runs directly on the user's device.
The core value proposition is providing private, on-device AI capabilities for coding assistance.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command to streamline the setup and execution of coding tools.
It enables running tools like Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex using both local and cloud models.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is integrating OpenAI's gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models for safety classification tasks.
The models, available in 20B and 120B sizes, are being brought to users through a partnership with OpenAI and ROOST.
The models are permissively licensed under the Apache 2.0 license, promoting open use.

stories.title: Amazon is adding a vertical video feed to Prime Video
stories.author: Emma Roth
bullet_points: Amazon Prime Video is integrating a vertical, short-form video feed called "Clips."
This move positions Prime Video to compete with short-form video trends popularized by platforms like TikTok.
The feed allows users to sample content and easily transition to renting or viewing the full title.

stories.title: Course correction: Google to link more sources in AI Overviews
stories.author: Ryan Whitwam
bullet_points: Google is enhancing its AI Overviews feature by improving source citation.
The update aims to provide users with more transparent and traceable information.
This represents a key course correction in Google's generative AI search strategy.

stories.title: ActCam: Zero-Shot Joint Camera and 3D Motion Control for Video Generation
stories.author: Baptiste Bellot-Gurlet
bullet_points: ActCam is a zero-shot method designed for fine-grained control in video generation, addressing both character motion and cinematography.
It jointly transfers character motion from a source video into a new scene while allowing per-frame control over camera parameters (intrinsic and extrinsic).
The model is built upon existing pretrained image-to-video diffusion models, conditioned on scene depth and other inputs.

stories.title: AI Co-Mathematician: Accelerating Mathematicians with Agentic AI
stories.author: Pushmeet Kohli
bullet_points: Introduction of an 'AI Co-Mathematician' workbench designed to assist mathematicians in open-ended research.
The system leverages agentic AI to provide holistic support across the entire mathematical workflow, from ideation to theorem proving.
Key features include an asynchronous, stateful workspace capable of managing uncertainty and tracking failed hypotheses.

stories.title: ActCam: Zero-Shot Joint Camera and 3D Motion Control for Video Generation
stories.author: Baptiste Bellot-Gurlet
bullet_points: ActCam is a zero-shot method designed for fine-grained control in video generation, addressing both character motion and camera trajectory.
It allows for the joint transfer of character motion from a source video into a novel scene.
The system enables per-frame control over intrinsic and extrinsic camera parameters, building upon existing image-to-video diffusion models.

stories.title: Why Global LLM Leaderboards Are Misleading: Small Portfolios for Heterogeneous Supervised ML
stories.author: Swati Gupta
bullet_points: Current LLM leaderboards, which rely on pairwise human feedback (like Arena), are shown to be misleading.
Analysis of 89K comparisons across 52 LLMs reveals that the top models are statistically indistinguishable.
The study suggests that the global Bradley-Terry ranking fails because a large proportion of decisive votes cancel each other out.

stories.title: Cited but Not Verified: Parsing and Evaluating Source Attribution in LLM Deep Research Agents
stories.author: Corey Feld
bullet_points: LLM-powered research agents face a critical flaw: their citations cannot be reliably verified.
Existing methods (self-citation or RAG) fail to validate source accessibility, relevance, or factual consistency.
The paper introduces a novel source attribution evaluation framework using an AST parser to address this verification gap.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage Claude Code capabilities within Ollama's ecosystem.
It bridges the gap between open-source local models and proprietary, advanced models like Claude.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex functionality allows models to read, modify, and execute code within a user's directory.
The integration supports various open-weight alternatives, such as gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: Here is Yarbo’s promise to fix the robot mower that ran me over
stories.author: Sean Hollister
bullet_points: Robot lawnmowers from Yarbo have been found vulnerable to hacking.
The vulnerability allows hackers to access sensitive user data, including GPS coordinates and passwords.
Yarbo has issued a response or promise regarding a fix for the security flaws.

stories.title: The new Wild West of AI kids’ toys
stories.author: Sophie Charara, WIRED.com
bullet_points: New connected AI companions are emerging in the children's toy market.
These toys have the potential to significantly alter children's play and interaction styles.
The rapid development has prompted calls from lawmakers for potential regulation or bans.

stories.title: Manufacturing qubits that can move
stories.author: John Timmer
bullet_points: The article addresses the technical challenge of integrating electronic manufacturing with flexible geometries.
The focus is on the difficulty of manufacturing mobile or movable qubits.
This topic is highly relevant to the advancement and practical application of quantum computing hardware.

stories.title: UniPool: A Globally Shared Expert Pool for Mixture-of-Experts
stories.author: Hong Cheng
bullet_points: Introduces UniPool, a novel approach to Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) architectures.
Challenges the conventional MoE design where each transformer layer has its own isolated expert set.
Suggests that the rigid per-layer expert allocation rule may not be optimal, based on routing probe analysis.

stories.title: Verifier-Backed Hard Problem Generation for Mathematical Reasoning
stories.author: Ning Miao
bullet_points: LLMs excel at solving problems but struggle to generate novel, valid, and challenging problems for training.
The research introduces VHG, a method that uses verification to address the flaws in current problem generation techniques.
VHG aims to overcome limitations of human-dependent or naive self-play methods that often result in invalid problems.

stories.title: When No Benchmark Exists: Validating Comparative LLM Safety Scoring Without Ground-Truth Labels
stories.author: Michael A. Riegler
bullet_points: Addresses the critical problem of comparing LLM safety when no established, labeled benchmarks exist for a specific context.
Introduces the concept of 'benchmarkless comparative safety scoring' for real-world deployment auditing.
Defines strict conditions (fixed scenario pack, rubric, auditor, etc.) under which scenario-based audits can serve as valid deployment evidence.

stories.title: Superintelligent Retrieval Agent: The Next Frontier of Information Retrieval
stories.author: Anshumali Shrivastava
bullet_points: Current retrieval-augmented agents often treat information retrieval as a 'black box' process.
Existing methods rely on iterative, exploratory querying, mimicking a novice user's search pattern.
The proposed advancement aims to move beyond black-box retrieval by incorporating expert-like priors for more efficient information navigation.

stories.title: Are We Making Progress in Multimodal Domain Generalization? A Comprehensive Benchmark Study
stories.author: Olga Fink
bullet_points: The study addresses the ambiguity in Multimodal Domain Generalization (MMDG) progress, questioning if reported gains are genuine or due to inconsistent evaluation.
Current MMDG research is noted as fragmented, exhibiting significant variation across datasets, modality configurations, and experimental setups.
Existing benchmarks are criticized for being too narrow, focusing heavily on action recognition while neglecting other critical real-world applications.

stories.title: StraTA: Incentivizing Agentic Reinforcement Learning with Strategic Trajectory Abstraction
stories.author: Zhenfei Yin
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of optimizing LLM-based agents for long-horizon decision-making, where current methods are too reactive.
Introduces StraTA (Strategic Trajectory Abstraction), a framework that integrates explicit trajectory-level strategy into agentic RL.
Aims to improve exploration and credit assignment over extended decision trajectories in complex AI systems.

stories.title: UniPool: A Globally Shared Expert Pool for Mixture-of-Experts
stories.author: Hong Cheng
bullet_points: Proposes UniPool, a novel architecture for Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) models.
Challenges the conventional MoE design where each transformer layer has isolated expert capacity.
Suggests that replacing layer-specific routing with uniform random routing has a minimal impact on accuracy, indicating potential for more efficient expert sharing.

stories.title: Verifier-Backed Hard Problem Generation for Mathematical Reasoning
stories.author: Ning Miao
bullet_points: LLMs excel at solving problems but struggle to generate novel, valid, and challenging problems for training.
The research introduces VHG, a method that uses verification to generate hard mathematical reasoning problems.
VHG aims to overcome limitations in existing methods, which rely on costly human experts or prone-to-failure self-play.

stories.title: Optimizer-Model Consistency: Full Finetuning with the Same Optimizer as Pretraining Forgets Less
stories.author: Tong Zhang
bullet_points: The research investigates the role of optimizers in the pretraining and finetuning of Large Language Models (LLMs).
The study observes that using the same optimizer during full finetuning as was used during pretraining leads to a better learning-forgetting tradeoff.
This method reportedly outperforms other optimizers and LoRA during Supervised Finetuning (SFT) by forgetting less while maintaining performance.

stories.title: When No Benchmark Exists: Validating Comparative LLM Safety Scoring Without Ground-Truth Labels
stories.author: Michael A. Riegler
bullet_points: Addresses the critical problem of comparing LLM safety when no established, labeled benchmarks exist for a specific context.
Introduces the concept of 'benchmarkless comparative safety scoring' to formalize this evaluation setting.
Defines strict conditions (fixed scenario pack, rubric, auditor, etc.) under which scenario-based audits can serve as deployment evidence.

stories.title: Superintelligent Retrieval Agent: The Next Frontier of Information Retrieval
stories.author: Anshumali Shrivastava
bullet_points: Current retrieval-augmented agents often treat knowledge base retrieval as a 'black box' process.
Existing methods rely on iterative, exploratory querying, mimicking a novice user's search pattern.
The proposed advancement aims to move beyond black-box retrieval by incorporating expert-like priors to improve efficiency and reduce latency.

stories.title: Are We Making Progress in Multimodal Domain Generalization? A Comprehensive Benchmark Study
stories.author: Olga Fink
bullet_points: The paper addresses the lack of clarity regarding genuine progress in Multimodal Domain Generalization (MMDG).
Current MMDG research suffers from fragmentation due to inconsistent evaluation protocols across studies.
Existing benchmarks are criticized for being overly focused on action recognition, neglecting broader real-world applications.

stories.title: EMO: Pretraining Mixture of Experts for Emergent Modularity
stories.author: Sewon Min
bullet_points: The paper addresses a limitation of current Large Language Models (LLMs) being monolithic, requiring the full model for narrow tasks.
Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) models are proposed as a solution to improve efficiency by activating only relevant experts.
The research tackles the practical issue where restricting inference to a subset of experts often causes significant performance degradation.

stories.title: Verifier-Backed Hard Problem Generation for Mathematical Reasoning
stories.author: Ning Miao
bullet_points: LLMs excel at solving math problems but struggle to generate novel, valid, and challenging problems for training.
The research introduces VHG, a method designed to generate hard mathematical problems backed by formal verification.
VHG aims to overcome limitations of current methods, which rely on expensive human experts or prone-to-failure self-play.

stories.title: When No Benchmark Exists: Validating Comparative LLM Safety Scoring Without Ground-Truth Labels
stories.author: Michael A. Riegler
bullet_points: Addresses the critical problem of comparing LLM safety when no pre-existing labeled benchmarks are available.
Proposes a framework for 'benchmarkless comparative safety scoring' for real-world deployments.
Defines strict conditions (fixed scenario pack, rubric, auditor, etc.) under which scenario-based audits can serve as deployment evidence.

stories.title: StraTA: Incentivizing Agentic Reinforcement Learning with Strategic Trajectory Abstraction
stories.author: Zhenfei Yin
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of long-horizon decision-making in LLM-based agents, which are often purely reactive.
Introduces StraTA (Strategic Trajectory Abstraction), a framework to incorporate explicit trajectory-level strategy into agentic RL.
Aims to improve exploration and credit assignment over extended decision-making processes.

stories.title: Recursive Agent Optimization
stories.author: Graham Neubig
bullet_points: Introduces Recursive Agent Optimization (RAO), a new reinforcement learning approach.
RAO is designed to train 'recursive agents' capable of spawning and delegating sub-tasks to themselves.
This recursive structure enables agents to scale to longer contexts and tackle complex problems using a divide-and-conquer strategy.

stories.title: Can RL Teach Long-Horizon Reasoning to LLMs? Expressiveness Is Key
stories.author: Abulhair Saparov
bullet_points: Addresses a key limitation in applying Reinforcement Learning (RL) to Large Language Models (LLMs): the lack of controlled, scalable environments for studying training scaling.
Introduces 'ScaleLogic,' a new synthetic logical reasoning framework designed to independently control two critical axes of difficulty.
The framework allows researchers to systematically vary the required proof planning depth (horizon) and the expressiveness of the underlying logic.

stories.title: MASPO: Joint Prompt Optimization for LLM-based Multi-Agent Systems
stories.author: Min Zhang
bullet_points: Introduces MASPO, a novel framework for optimizing prompts in LLM-based Multi-Agent Systems (MAS).
Addresses the challenge of aligning local agent objectives with overall system goals in collaborative tasks.
Focuses on the difficulty of jointly optimizing role-specific prompts across interacting agents.

stories.title: Why Most Browser AI Demos Fail on Real Hardware
stories.author: bruno_juca_7038c22bcca1db
bullet_points: The article addresses the gap between AI demos running on powerful benchmark machines and their performance on standard, real-world hardware.
It focuses on the technical challenge of building adaptive local AI inference solutions.
The core theme is moving AI deployment from idealized testing environments to practical, constrained hardware.

stories.title: Bulletproofing LLM Structured Output in Python: Healing Retries, Cost Caps, and Drift Detection (Runnable Code)
stories.author: velsof
bullet_points: Focuses on practical engineering challenges for production LLM deployment.
Addresses critical issues like healing retries, cost capping, and output drift detection.
Provides runnable code examples, making the technical discussion highly actionable for developers.
